    Atrey Iyer

    Engineer · high school senior · diver · builder.

    • Chicago, IL
    • Email

    Atrey Iyer

    I’m an Indian American high school senior and an engineer. I work across research, software, and hardware — and I like building things that get used in the real world.

    Right now I’m a Mechanical Engineering Research Intern at Northwestern University, exploring agentic AI and its applications in mechanical engineering, where I’m in the process of co-authoring a research paper. I’ve also interned as a Process Mining & Data Automation Intern at Peech, and as an Electrical Engineering Project Intern with the County Civic Engineering Initiative, where my team designed an autonomous trash-skimming boat.

    Outside of internships, I captain my school’s Varsity Math Modeling Team — we won the national Modeling the Future Challenge and were semifinalists in the MathWorks M3 Challenge — and I lead development in my school’s Computer Science Club. I dive for the varsity team as co-captain, and I tutor advanced math at Mathnasium.

    Have a look around: my projects, publications, tutoring, and full CV are in the menu above.

    Highlights

    • 1st Place, National Champion — Modeling the Future Challenge (MTFC), $25,000 grand prize
    • International Semifinalist — MathWorks Math Modeling (M3) Challenge (top 12 of 780)
    • Published Author — Chicago Section ACS Chemical Bulletin
    • National Qualifier — Business Professionals of America (BPA) & TEAMS Engineering
    • IHSA Student-Athlete Academic Award & MVP athlete honors

    Want to get in touch? Email me at iyeratrey@gmail.com.
